inspirationMapping.takeaway: Give each content card a dominant pastel background color drawn from a 1950s appliance palette and use rounded silhouette shapes that echo retro appliance curves.
implementation: Fridge magnet ideas use four distinct pastel tints (avocado green, harvest gold, cream, copper) as background variants, each evoking a different SMEG appliance color
Recipe box project cards have colored filing tab accents at the top in avocado green, harvest gold, and copper brown
All buttons and inputs use generous border-radius (8-14px) creating pill/capsule shapes that echo the rounded silhouettes of 1950s kitchen appliances
Dashboard stat cards use rounded 16px corners with soft shadows, mimicking the friendly rounded edges of retro appliance panels
inspirationMapping.differentiation: Where SMEG uses bold saturated product photography against white backgrounds, this pass uses warm cream (#faf5e8) as the base with hand-lettered Pacifico headings to create a homemade-recipe feel rather than a retail product showcase. The pastel tints are applied to card surfaces rather than hero banners.

inspirationMapping.takeaway: Add a horizontal color-swatch strip as a navigation or filtering element, and give buttons and interactive elements a subtle chrome gradient sheen simulating polished kitchen appliance trim.
implementation: Settings view includes a horizontal color swatch selector strip for kitchen theme colors, directly inspired by KitchenAid's mixer color selection strips
Appliance dial knobs in settings use radial-gradient from white to cream-dark to create a chrome/brushed-metal visual effect
Toggle switch knobs have an inset notch detail (::after pseudo-element) mimicking real appliance dial indicators
Recipe box category tabs act as a horizontal navigation strip reminiscent of KitchenAid's product category navigation
inspirationMapping.differentiation: KitchenAid centers the appliance as hero against solid-color backgrounds. This pass distributes the appliance metaphor across functional UI controls — the dial, switches, and swatch selectors are working interactive elements rather than decorative hero imagery. The chrome treatment is subtle (radial gradients on knobs) rather than edge-to-edge metallic surfaces.

inspirationMapping.takeaway: Set the page on a warm cream background and present content items as still-life compositions with editorial captions, using copper/terracotta accents and generous card gutters to create the orderly warmth of a retro kitchen shelf display.
implementation: Page background is warm cream (#faf5e8), very close to Food52's signature #FAF7F2, creating the same kitchen-warm base tone
Copper (#b87333) is used extensively as the accent material — input focus rings, push-pin icons, entity headers, file icons — evoking Food52's cast-iron and ceramic cookware warmth
Card grids use generous 20px gutters with orderly arrangement, creating the curated editorial shelf display feel
Recipe cards include descriptive captions with ingredient stories and cooking context, mimicking Food52's editorial recipe card voice rather than just listing titles
inspirationMapping.differentiation: Food52 mixes warm photography with clean card frames for a food-magazine editorial feel. This pass replaces photography with interactive kitchen widgets (gauges, dials, timers) and hand-lettered Pacifico headings to create a more playful, handmade recipe-box aesthetic rather than a polished editorial publication.

overallApproach: This pass synthesizes the retro kitchen appliance aesthetic by treating the entire UI as a kitchen workspace. SMEG's pastel coloring strategy is applied to content cards and magnets. KitchenAid's chrome hardware language is embedded in interactive dial/switch controls. Food52's warm cream editorial foundation sets the overall tone. The result feels like opening grandma's kitchen to find a modern recipe management system built into the appliances themselves.
